task.title: Fix reconciliation lifecycle consumption
task.summary: Make graph reconciliation stop treating accepted builds as perpetual drift once downstream knowledge, roadmap, implementation, and validation evidence have consumed them.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki status reports reconciliation drift only for real unconsumed handoffs, not for completed historical builds.
acceptance: Accepted feedback builds with linked documentation builds no longer produce documentation-loop drift.
Accepted documentation builds with linked roadmap/implementation evidence no longer produce roadmap/documentation drift.
Validated or accepted implementation evidence closes the corresponding reconciliation route.
codewiki_state after refresh no longer reports the current false reconciliation warning for already completed work.
Tests cover consumed feedback, documentation, and implementation build reconciliation behavior.
non_goals: Persist reconciliation truth outside canonical sources.
Recreate removed queue files or deprecated aliases.
verification: codewiki_state refresh shows no false reconciliation drift for completed builds.

summary: Implemented reconciliation lifecycle consumption in the graph so accepted feedback/documentation builds are considered aligned when downstream documentation, roadmap changes, implementation evidence, or validation evidence reference them. Updated tests and KB docs, and added a drift-audit validation report for historical absorbed handoffs.
checks_run: graph reconciliation audit: no accepted feedback/documentation build false drifts remain

evidence: Added smoke assertions for consumed feedback builds, consumed documentation builds, unconsumed feedback builds, unconsumed documentation builds, and validated implementation builds.
notes: Regression coverage remains in smoke tests because reconciliation is exercised through real tool calls and graph rebuilds.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: Added build reference normalization, downstream build indexes, multi-source validation matching, lifecycle completion checks, and build-derived edges in graph.ts.
notes: No generated graph hand edits; .codewiki/index_graph.json was regenerated from current source.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: Added smoke assertions for consumed feedback builds, consumed documentation builds, unconsumed feedback builds, unconsumed documentation builds, and validated implementation builds.
code_change_evidence: Added build reference normalization, downstream build indexes, multi-source validation matching, lifecycle completion checks, and build-derived edges in graph.ts.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Accepted feedback builds with linked documentation builds no longer produce documentation-loop drift.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: graph.ts indexes documentation builds by source_feedback_build and treats feedback builds as consumed when downstream documentation, implementation, or validation evidence references them; tests assert consumed feedback no longer appears in reconciliation.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Accepted documentation builds with linked roadmap/implementation evidence no longer produce roadmap/documentation drift.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: graph.ts treats documentation builds as consumed when roadmap_changes exist, implementation evidence links to them, validation passes, or source feedback is knowledge-only; tests assert roadmap-backed documentation is consumed.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Validated or accepted implementation evidence closes the corresponding reconciliation route.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: graph.ts recognizes validation_refs, validation_verdict.pass, validation source/sources, and task-linked pass reports; tests assert validated implementation builds do not stay in reconciliation.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: codewiki_state after refresh no longer reports the current false reconciliation warning for already completed work.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Current-source graph rebuild reports only the open TASK-054 and dirty-worktree code/documentation items; accepted historical feedback/documentation build false drifts are cleared.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Tests cover consumed feedback, documentation, and implementation build reconciliation behavior.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: tests/smoke/builds.test.mjs and scripts/smoke-test.mjs updated; npm test passes.

risks: Live Pi tools still use the previous loaded extension until Pi reload; current-source rebuild validates the patched code.

handoff.fallback: Use codewiki_state refresh=true and this implementation_build; do not rely on chat transcript memory.

commit.title: chore(codewiki): record TASK-054 Fix reconciliation lifecycle consumption implementation evidence
commit.body:
Implemented reconciliation lifecycle consumption in the graph so accepted feedback/documentation builds are considered aligned when downstream documentation, roadmap changes, implementation evidence, or validation evidence reference them. Updated tests and KB docs, and added a drift-audit validation report for historical absorbed handoffs.

Checks: npx tsc --noEmit, node scripts/check-architecture.mjs, npm test, current-source CodewikiRebuilder rebuildAll, graph reconciliation audit: no accepted feedback/documentation build false drifts remain
Validation: .codewiki/validation/2026-05-11-task-054-implementation-pass.json, .codewiki/validation/2026-05-11-reconciliation-lifecycle-drift-audit-pass.json
Remote publication requires explicit approval; this build is recommendation-only.
